bullets: IRPC's July cargo fell 90-120: homo to 1,260, Film 1126NK to 1,350 CFR.
Jinneng yarn eased to ~1,180 CIF; NSRP local equates to ~1,130-1,140 CIF.
Domestic NSRP 114G 33,500 June / 33,000 July (32,800 low); T3034 flat 34,000.
Petrochina LLDPE to 1,100 (deals 1,070-1,080); Lotte UL814 down 150 to 1,160.
Lotte LDPE LF512/410/311 finalized at 1,300 CIF, among the market lows.
Lotte Titan held HDPE Film 1,280, trimmed blow to 1,320; few deals done.
AGC trimmed K66/K58 another 20 on Jun 17 to 730-740 CIF for 500t+ lots.
Yihua SG-5 to 21,500; oil-based PVC down to 22,500-23,000 VND/kg.
SCG SG660 to 23,600 (-400); Formosa S65 still above 25,000, not priced to compete.

editorial.changedNote: 9 of 50 moved (8 down, 1 up), led by PP & PVC

summary: International PE offers softened further; sellers cut to clear inventory while buyers held back and trading stayed thin.
Petrochina LLDPE to 1,100 (deals 1,070-1,080); Lotte UL814 -150 to 1,160; LDPE LF512/410/311 finalized 1,300 CIF.
Lotte Titan held HDPE Film 1,280 and trimmed blow to 1,320; domestic HDPE blow (LSP) 38,800 south / 39,500 north.
paras: International PE offers to Vietnam softened further this week as buyers held back and sellers cut prices to clear circulating inventory. With most distributors trying to draw stock down to low levels and avoid holding high-cost cargo, many commercial sellers accepted sharp discounts despite limited spot availability. Trading stayed thin, with buyers largely indifferent and declining to bid at current offer levels. Unless otherwise noted, import prices below are CIF Vietnam in USD/ton.
LLDPE:

The cheapest LLDPE on the market is Chinese-origin material. Petrochina's LLDPE Film was offered at USD 1,100/ton, down 20–30 USD/ton week-on-week, with some domestic buyers reporting that deals could close materially lower, around USD 1,070–1,080/ton. Lotte Chemical Korea's LLDPE injection grade UL814 fell sharply, closing to Vietnamese buyers at USD 1,160/ton — down 150 USD/ton from early June. Separately, an unconfirmed report suggests Sabic (Saudi Arabia) may continue selling off unsold LLDPE grades (July 2026 lot) at just USD 1,100/ton during June, though this still requires verification.
HDPE:

Lotte Titan Malaysia had offered July cargo at USD 1,280/ton for HDPE Film and USD 1,330/ton for HDPE blow last week. This week the distributor held HDPE Film flat at USD 1,280/ton and trimmed HDPE blow by 10 USD/ton to USD 1,320/ton, while signaling openness to further negotiation — yet almost no deals were recorded as buyers stayed on the sidelines. In practice, numerous HDPE Film/yarn grades from Lotte Hyundai, SK Chemical and Hanwha (Korea) and from STL and BASF (China) have sold into Vietnam at USD 1,150–1,200/ton, and one major northern manufacturer said it received HDPE Film quotes from commercial sellers starting as low as USD 1,120/ton.
LDPE:

Closing sale prices for Lotte Hyundai Korea's LDPE Film grades LF512, LF410 and LF311 reached Vietnamese buyers at USD 1,300/ton for July delivery — among the lowest in the market and a source of strong competitive pressure.
Domestic market (VND/kg, VAT included):

LSP has nearly cleared its PP and PE inventory after focusing on exports during the recent one-month furnace shutdown. This week LSP offered only a small volume of HDPE blow — 38,800 VND/kg in the south and 39,500 VND/kg in the north — with few deals concluded; some commercial sellers continued to quote LSP PE well below those levels, and LSP's distribution office was also marketing Chinese-origin HDPE. Spreads between distributors remain wide: HDPE yarn 3301 (SK Chemical, Korea) was offered at 36,500 VND/kg and HDPE yarn MF5000 (Lotte Hyundai, Korea) at 37,500 VND/kg, down 500 VND/kg from late last week. Middle Eastern HDPE Film was commonly quoted at 37,000–38,000 VND/kg, while one large customer reportedly imported HDPE FS7000 (STL, China) at just 34,800 VND/kg — though smaller buyers were still seeing offers around 36,000 VND/kg.

In LDPE, a leading distributor offered LDPE Film 260GG (Lotte Titan, Malaysia) at 45,000 VND/kg, below most commercial offers; LDPE 2426H (Petrochina, China) was commonly quoted at 44,000–44,500 VND/kg, though some sell-off deals were recorded at just 40,000–40,500 VND/kg. In LLDPE, even premium Middle Eastern grades such as Sabic's 218WJ and Qamar's FD21HS traded at 35,000 VND/kg, while Chinese LLDPE Film was offered at 34,500 VND/kg but had been transacting since last week around 33,000 VND/kg or lower.

summary: PP offers fell again, led by Thai and Chinese cuts, though homopolymer's decline began to slow as margins thinned.
IRPC's July cargo down 90-120: homo 1,260, 1126NK 1,350 CFR; Jinneng yarn ~1,180 CIF; NSRP local ~1,130-1,140.
Domestic: NSRP 114G 33,500 June / 33,000 July (32,800 low); BSR T3034 flat 34,000; copo 36,000-38,000 VND/kg.
paras: PP offers to Vietnam fell further this week, led by sharp cuts on Thai and Chinese cargo, though the decline in PP homopolymer began to slow as producers and distributors tried to defend already-thin margins. Higher-priced specialty grades kept easing while buying interest stayed weak, and both sides preferred to keep inventories low rather than carry stock. Import prices below are in USD/ton on the basis indicated (CFR, CIF or FOB).
Imported offers (USD/ton):

IRPC (Thailand) offered its July 2026 PP and PS cargo at sharply lower levels, down 90–120 USD/ton from early June: PP homopolymer fell to USD 1,260/ton CFR Ho Chi Minh City, PP Film 1126NK to USD 1,350/ton CFR, PP 1100RC to USD 1,310/ton CFR, and B1111 extrusion to USD 1,370/ton CFR. Even after the cuts, IRPC remained relatively high versus the broader market. From Korea, a Lotte Chemical distributor offered PP Film H4540 at USD 1,330/ton CIF — 20 USD/ton below IRPC's film — while Lotte Chemical's raffia grades M9600 and L270A stayed elevated at USD 1,500/ton CIF, little changed on the month; Lotte Hyundai had earlier closed PP yarn to some Vietnamese buyers as low as USD 1,170/ton CIF.

Chinese material remained the cheapest: Jinneng's PP yarn/injection eased to USD 1,140/ton FOB China (roughly USD 1,180/ton CIF Vietnam), down 35 USD/ton week-on-week, with BOPP correspondingly at USD 1,150/ton FOB; general Chinese PP offers fell 30–40 USD/ton to USD 1,180–1,190/ton FOB, yet still drew little interest. Domestically referenced levels were lower still — NSRP's PP yarn for the local market equated to about USD 1,130–1,140/ton CIF — and with more Korean cargo on offer, PP homopolymer deals slipped to USD 1,170–1,180/ton CIF.
Domestic market (VND/kg, VAT included):

Marubeni, NSRP's lead distributor, offered NSRP PP 114G at 33,500 VND/kg for June delivery and 33,000 VND/kg for July, though buyers stayed cautious in placing orders; the lowest commercial deal for 114G since late last week was recorded at 32,800 VND/kg. BSR's PP T3034, after a steep drop the prior week, held flat at 34,000 VND/kg.

In copolymer, PP copo — including Hyosung Vina's domestic output and imported material — traded broadly at 36,000–38,000 VND/kg. Lotte Korea's raffia grade M9600 ranged 46,000–47,000 VND/kg, while PP Film from Korea, India and Thailand was common at 41,000–42,000 VND/kg, down from the prior week.

summary: PVC weakened with import and domestic cuts into thin demand; carbide-based held up better than oil-based grades.
AGC cut K66/K58 a further 20 on Jun 17 morning to 730-740 CIF for 500t+ orders, though buyers stayed cautious.
Domestic: Yihua SG-5 21,500; oil-based 22,500-23,000; SCG SG660 23,600 (-400); AGC Vina PM66R ~21,816-22,248 incl VAT.
paras: PVC offers to Vietnam also weakened this week, with import sellers and domestic distributors alike cutting prices into thin demand. Carbide-based (coal) PVC held its value better than oil-based grades, which saw the steepest commercial cuts, while buyers committed only cautiously even at the lower levels. Import prices below are CIF Vietnam in USD/ton.
Imported offers (USD/ton):

AGC Group had offered PVC K66 (July 2026 lot) from its Vinythai (Thailand, AVT) and Asahimas (Indonesia, ASC) plants at USD 750–760/ton CIF depending on volume, with orders above 1,000 tons quoted at USD 740/ton CIF — though the cargo had drawn limited buying. On the morning of 17 June, the distributor announced a further 20 USD/ton discount on all K66 and K58 grades for customers taking 500 tons or more, cutting AGC's PVC to USD 730–740/ton CIF; even so, few buyers were ready to commit to 500-ton-plus lots at that level.
Domestic market (VND/kg, VAT included):

On the commercial market, a leading distributor offered Yihua's PVC SG-5 at 21,500 VND/kg at Yen Vien (Hanoi), down 200 VND/kg week-on-week for end-June delivery — evidence that carbide-based PVC has held up better and traded more steadily than oil-based material. Oil-based PVC, by contrast, saw heavier cuts, with offers commonly down to 22,500–23,000 VND/kg, only about 1,000 VND/kg above carbide-based grades; one large distributor offered AGC Vinythai's PVC 266RC (Thailand) as low as 22,200 VND/kg in both the north and south.

Among other Thai cargo, a large northern distributor offered SCG's PVC SG660 at 23,600 VND/kg, down 400 VND/kg from last week, though trade was slow; Formosa Taiwan's PVC S65 was still offered above 25,000 VND/kg but aimed mainly at long-standing customers rather than priced to compete broadly. Separately, AGC's local plant AGC Vina (ACNV) offered PVC PM66R (July 2026 lot) to the domestic market at 20,400–20,600 VND/kg excluding VAT for medium and small buyers and 20,200 VND/kg excluding VAT for the largest — equivalent to 21,816–22,248 VND/kg with 8% VAT, for delivery in the south.
